response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine staff-level credentials and a long track record of shipping production software, but the resume does not evidence the depth of AI/GenAI expertise this role requires. The job demands 8+ years of AI-driven solution development; the only AI-adjacent work on record is roughly six months at re:cinq (April–October 2025) involving MCP code generators and agentic tooling, followed by a current career gap. Python — the de-facto language for AI/ML engineering — is absent from his skills list. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. Cloud experience is present but narrow (AWS CDK in a pricing context), not AI infrastructure. The breadth is real, but the GenAI depth the role requires at staff level is simply not demonstrated.
strengths: 15+ years of full-stack engineering across diverse stacks with documented staff-level and technical-leadership experience
Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and agentic-workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq, directly relevant to the role's toolchain
Strong TypeScript/Node.js fundamentals with AWS CDK exposure and a history of building and operating production distributed systems
concerns: Python is not listed anywhere in skills or employment history — a critical gap for production AI/ML engineering at staff level
GenAI experience is extremely thin (~6 months, vaguely described) with no evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability, or multi-agent frameworks
Currently in a career gap since October 2025 with only a brief AI-adjacent tenure on record, raising questions about readiness for an immediate staff-level deployment role
